description: Multi-tenant MSSP portal with SOC operations, RMM integration, compliance automation, client lifecycle management, and an AG-UI SSE integration builder. PostgreSQL RLS enforcement, 30+ RBAC roles, HMAC-SHA256 licensing engine. 8 fully-built portal sections with live workflow data, global cookie-based client context switcher, @kit/integrations package with OCSF 1.3.0 normalizer, ConnectWise/NinjaRMM/Stripe connectors, and Azure Speech voice mode. 123 UAT tests written.
sprint5_summary: Sprint 5 (Integration Builder) ships AG-UI SSE chat endpoint (POST /api/integrations/builder) with 4 tools, @kit/integrations package (IntegrationConnector interface + ConnectWise, NinjaRMM, Stripe connectors, OcsfNormalizer mapping to OCSF 1.3.0 classes 2001/3002/6003), integrations + integration_events DB schema with full RLS, integration builder page with D3 force-directed topology graph, and Azure Speech SDK voice mode (STT + TTS) with graceful fallback. New route: /home/integrations. 5 checkpoints (CP-45 through CP-49). Quality gates: typecheck pass, lint pass, build pass.
sprint4_summary: Sprint 4 (Multi-Tenant Client Scoping) implements cookie-based global client context switcher (active_client_id / active_client_name cookies), ClientContextProvider + setActiveClient server actions, ClientSwitcher header dropdown, optional clientId filter on all list/stats queries, getClientKpis() aggregate action, per-section context banners (SOC, RMM, Billing, Support), and Clients list v3 with per-client live KPI badges and View Client navigation. 10 checkpoints (CP-35 through CP-44). Quality gates: typecheck pass, lint pass, build pass.
sprint3_summary: Sprint 3 (Workflows) delivers live data across all 7 portal sections: RMM live endpoint inventory table, SOC SLA deadline badges (overdue/warning/ok) with analyst assignment, Clients onboarding status badges and service tier pills, Billing invoice creation modal with full CRUD. 4 DB migrations (rmm_endpoints, alert_source_format, sla/onboarding enums), 3 new server action groups in @kit/mssp package, 123 Playwright UAT tests. Quality gates: typecheck 0 errors, lint 0 errors (3 pre-existing warnings), build PASS.
sprint2_summary: Sprint 2 delivers all 7 MSSP portal section pages (SOC, Compliance, RMM, Clients, Billing, Admin, Support) backed by PostgreSQL schemas, RLS policies, and server actions for each domain. Bonus: Docker containerization (multi-stage Dockerfile, docker-compose, Nginx, NATS JetStream) and Supabase MCP integration for schema-aware development. 15 stories shipped with full typecheck 16/16, lint 14/14, build pass, and all 8 routes live-tested via Puppeteer.

completed_sprints.summary: Multi-tenant schema, CI/CD pipeline, Terraform IaC, RBAC (20 roles), licensing engine (HMAC-SHA256), comprehensive RLS audit, client server actions, and MSSP home dashboard.

wave_1.title: Wave 1 — Foundation (DONE)
wave_1.description: Multi-tenant schema (accounts_extensions + clients tables with ltree hierarchy), GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline (typecheck → lint → build), Terraform Azure workspace with remote state, MSSP portal navigation (6 sections: SOC, Compliance, RMM, Clients, Billing, Admin).

wave_2.title: Wave 2 — RBAC + Licensing (DONE)
wave_2.description: RBAC schema: mssp_role enum (20 roles from platform_owner to report_viewer), roles/permissions/account_role_assignments tables, check_mssp_admin_role() SECURITY DEFINER helper. Licensing engine (@kit/licensing): HMAC-SHA256 offline key validation, 4 service tiers (Starter/Professional/Enterprise/Sovereign), seat and endpoint quota enforcement.

wave_3.title: Wave 3 — RLS Audit (DONE)
wave_3.description: Comprehensive RLS policy patch: idempotent DO block enables RLS on all public schema tables, full SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies on accounts_extensions and clients via accounts_memberships, account_role_assignments write-restricted to mssp_admin+, immutable timestamp triggers, migration-time verification report.

wave_4.title: Wave 4 — Client Actions + Dashboard (DONE)
wave_4.description: Client management server actions (createClient, listClients, updateClient) with Zod validation and ActionResult<T> discriminated unions. MSSP home dashboard: 6-card portal overview (SOC, Compliance, RMM, Clients, Billing, Admin), server component, responsive grid, Active/Coming Soon badges.

wave_5.title: Wave 5 — Intelligence Layer (IN PROGRESS)
wave_5.description: Integration Builder shipped (Sprint 5): AG-UI SSE endpoint, @kit/integrations package with ConnectWise/NinjaRMM/Stripe connectors, OCSF 1.3.0 normalizer, D3 topology graph, Azure Speech voice mode. Remaining planned: Azure OpenAI for alert triage summarization, UEBA behavioral analytics, automated compliance reports, vCISO advisory tooling.

internal.headline: Building 3VS Vantage's Operating Platform
internal.pitch: Vantage Portal is the operational backbone of 3VS Vantage's managed security business. Built on Elixir/Phoenix for fault-tolerant event processing, NATS JetStream for decoupled service communication, and Next.js 15 for a responsive analyst experience — this platform replaces a patchwork of third-party tools with a unified, owned system. Every component is designed for multi-tenancy from day one, with PostgreSQL RLS enforcing strict isolation and 30+ RBAC roles spanning every operational persona.

slides.title: Architecture Overview
talking_points: Elixir/Phoenix backend handles all event ingestion, SOAR execution, and background jobs via OTP supervision trees
NATS JetStream provides the event spine — all services communicate through subject-scoped streams, not direct HTTP calls
Next.js 15 App Router frontend with Supabase Realtime for live incident and alert dashboards
PostgreSQL RLS enforces tenant isolation at the database layer — application bugs cannot leak cross-tenant data
slides.speaker_notes: Emphasize that the Elixir choice was deliberate for fault tolerance — a crashed SOAR worker doesn't take down the web tier. NATS gives us horizontal scalability without Kafka's operational overhead.

slides.title: RMM Integration & Endpoint Telemetry
talking_points: Tactical RMM bidirectional sync populates endpoint inventory, patch status, and agent health automatically
Webhook-driven real-time updates — no polling; endpoint state changes propagate within seconds
MeshCentral integration (Wave 2) will provide browser-based remote control proxied through the portal for full session audit logging
Custom field mapping allows MSSP-specific metadata to flow through without schema changes
slides.speaker_notes: Tactical RMM was chosen as the initial integration target due to its open API and strong MSP adoption. MeshCentral's self-hosted nature aligns with our security posture for remote access.

slides.title: SOC Operations & Incident Lifecycle
talking_points: Alert ingestion pipeline normalizes CEF, LEEF, syslog, and JSON from 50+ vendor parsers
Incident lifecycle from detection to closure with P1–P5 severity SLAs and automated breach notifications
SOAR playbook engine (Wave 2) uses Elixir GenServer state machines — playbooks are version-controlled and rollback-capable
Analyst workbench integrates MISP, OTX, and VirusTotal for threat enrichment without leaving the portal
slides.speaker_notes: The GenServer-based playbook engine gives us true fault isolation per playbook execution. If an enrichment step times out, the state machine can retry or branch without blocking other incidents.

slides.title: Compliance Targets: SOC 2 / ISO 27001
talking_points: Control mapping covers SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, NIST CSF, and CIS Controls v8
Evidence collection is automated from connected RMM and EDR sources — no manual uploads for covered controls
Immutable audit log with cryptographic integrity verification and 7-year retention for WORM compliance
Gap analysis engine generates remediation tasks directly linked to framework controls
slides.speaker_notes: Our compliance coverage is a direct competitive advantage — most MSSP platforms require a separate GRC tool. Owning this reduces client tool spend and increases our stickiness.

slides.title: Development Progress & Sprint Roadmap
talking_points: Sprint 1 (Foundation): Multi-tenancy, RBAC (20 roles), licensing engine (HMAC-SHA256), RLS audit — all 9 stories shipped
Sprint 2 (Portal Sections): All 7 MSSP portal pages live — SOC, Compliance, RMM, Clients, Billing, Admin, Support — 18 stories shipped
Sprint 3 (Workflows): Live data across all 7 sections — SLA badges, endpoint inventory, onboarding flows, invoice creation. 123 UAT tests. 10 stories shipped.

Sprint 4 (Multi-Tenant Client Scoping): Cookie-based global client context switcher, ClientSwitcher dropdown, per-section context banners, Clients list v3 with live KPI badges. 10 checkpoints shipped.

Sprint 5 (Integration Builder): AG-UI SSE chat endpoint, @kit/integrations package (ConnectWise/NinjaRMM/Stripe), OCSF 1.3.0 normalizer, D3 topology graph, Azure Speech voice mode. New /home/integrations route. 5 checkpoints shipped.
Sprint 6+ (planned): SOAR playbook engine, Azure OpenAI triage co-pilot, UEBA behavioral analytics, Tactical RMM sync, MeshCentral remote control
slides.speaker_notes: Five sprints complete: full portal operational with live workflow data, global client context, and an AI-native integration builder with voice input. The AG-UI SSE pattern positions us well for the SOAR playbook engine coming in Sprint 6+.

partner.headline: White-Label MSSP Platform for MSP Partners
partner.pitch: Vantage Portal offers MSP partners a fully white-labeled security operations platform they can resell under their own brand — without the multi-year investment of building one. Full CSS theming, custom domain routing, and co-branded email templates mean clients never see Vantage branding unless you want them to. A comprehensive REST and GraphQL API surface connects to the PSA tools your team already runs: ConnectWise, Autotask, and HaloPSA.

slides.title: White-Label Capabilities
talking_points: CSS variable theming system covers all UI surfaces — apply your brand palette, logo, and favicon through the admin panel
Per-tenant subdomain routing with automatic TLS provisioning — your clients access their portal at client.yourmssp.com
Branded PDF reports with your logo and color scheme — clients see your brand, not ours
Dark/light mode defaults configurable per tenant; admin live preview before publishing
slides.speaker_notes: The white-label system was designed co-first with the platform itself — not bolted on afterward. Every API response, email, and PDF runs through the tenant branding context.

slides.title: PSA Integration Surface
talking_points: Native ticket sync with ConnectWise Manage, Autotask PSA, and HaloPSA — incidents create tickets, tickets close incidents
Time entry synchronization for accurate billing against managed service agreements
Asset sync ensures PSA and portal inventory stay aligned without manual reconciliation
Configurable field mapping handles custom PSA schemas without code changes
slides.speaker_notes: PSA integration is table stakes for MSP adoption. We support the three dominant platforms by market share. Kaseya BMS and Syncro are on the roadmap based on partner demand.

slides.title: API Surface & Extensibility
talking_points: RESTful and GraphQL APIs cover 100% of platform capabilities — anything the UI can do, the API can do
Per-tenant API keys with scoped permissions allow partners to build custom integrations safely
Outbound webhooks with HMAC signature verification for real-time event delivery to partner systems
OpenAPI 3.1 spec auto-generated and published; Postman collection available for rapid integration development
slides.speaker_notes: The GraphQL layer is particularly valuable for partners building custom dashboards or mobile apps — they can fetch exactly the data shape they need in a single request.

slides.title: Onboarding & Client Provisioning
talking_points: Client onboarding wizard automates tenant creation, RMM agent deployment instructions, and initial asset import
License tier assignment determines feature availability — upsell paths are clear and self-service
Partner admin staff can manage multiple sub-tenants through a single pane without context-switching logins
Delegated admin model allows partners to grant clients scoped self-service access
slides.speaker_notes: Time-to-value for new clients is a key metric. The onboarding wizard targets under 30 minutes from contract signature to first alert ingesting.

slides.title: Co-Brand Potential & Revenue Share
talking_points: White-label tier partners can resell Vantage Portal under their own product name
Per-endpoint pricing model translates directly to MSP managed service agreement structures
Usage metering data available via API for partners to build their own billing overlays
Volume discounts at 500, 2000, and 10,000 managed endpoints
slides.speaker_notes: We want partners to succeed selling this as their own product. The commercial model is designed to make the Vantage margin invisible while keeping partner margins healthy.

investor.headline: The MSSP Operating System
investor.pitch: A unified platform for Managed Security Service Providers to run their entire business — RMM, SOC, billing, compliance, and client portal — from a single, AI-powered interface.

slides.title: Market Opportunity
talking_points: Global MSSP market exceeds $45B and is growing at 14% CAGR driven by SMB security outsourcing and regulatory pressure
Over 15,000 MSPs in North America are actively adding security services to their portfolio — creating immediate demand for MSSP tooling
Current tooling landscape is fragmented: MSSPs average 7–12 point solutions to replicate what Vantage Portal delivers in one platform
Compliance mandates (CMMC, FTC Safeguards Rule, state privacy laws) are forcing SMBs into MSSP relationships at an accelerating rate
slides.speaker_notes: The $45B figure covers pure-play MSSPs. When you include MSPs adding security services, the addressable market for MSSP tooling platforms exceeds $8B annually.

slides.title: Sticky SaaS Revenue Model
talking_points: Per-endpoint SaaS pricing aligns with how MSSPs sell to clients — revenue scales with customer growth automatically
Multi-year contracts standard in the MSP/MSSP channel: average contract length 2.3 years with high renewal rates
Platform switching costs are extremely high — RMM integrations, custom playbooks, and compliance evidence vaults create deep lock-in
Expansion revenue built in: as clients add endpoints or upgrade license tiers, ARPU grows without additional sales motion
slides.speaker_notes: The compliance evidence vault is particularly powerful for retention — a client leaving would need to migrate years of immutable audit records. This is a structural moat, not just a switching cost.

slides.title: Compliance Moat
talking_points: SOC 2, ISO 27001, NIST CSF, and CIS Controls coverage built into the platform — not an add-on module
Automated evidence collection eliminates the primary labor cost of compliance maintenance for MSSP clients
Compliance attestation becomes a competitive differentiator for MSSP sales cycles — clients choose the MSSP whose platform proves coverage
Regulatory expansion (DORA in EU, CMMC in US DoD supply chain) continuously expands the compliance addressable market
slides.speaker_notes: Competitors offer compliance bolt-ons at $15–30/endpoint/month. By building it into the platform, we make compliance a retention driver rather than an upsell that clients might skip.

slides.title: AI-Powered Differentiation
talking_points: Azure AI integration (Wave 5) enables alert triage summarization, reducing analyst mean time to respond by targeting 40%
UEBA behavioral analytics surfaces high-risk entities automatically — scaling analyst capacity without headcount growth
vCISO tooling allows MSSPs to offer advisory services at scale, opening a new revenue line without proportional cost
AI features are per-tenant token-budgeted — margin-positive at all license tiers
slides.speaker_notes: The AI layer is not window dressing — it directly addresses the #1 MSSP scaling problem: analyst shortage. Every alert summary and risk score our AI generates is an analyst-hour saved.

slides.title: Go-To-Market & Traction
talking_points: 3VS Vantage is the flagship customer and design partner — platform built against real MSSP operational requirements
White-label partner channel creates a multiplier: each MSP partner brings their own client base onto the platform
Initial target: 50 MSP partners in Year 1, each managing 200+ endpoints, reaching 10,000 endpoints under management
Channel sales through existing MSP distributor relationships accelerates partner acquisition without direct enterprise sales motion
slides.speaker_notes: Using 3VS Vantage as the anchor customer de-risks the product — every feature ships against a paying, demanding internal customer before partner release.
